2604.17148.title: Graph-of-Agents: A Graph-based Framework for Multi-Agent LLM Collaboration
2604.17148.paper_summary: With an ever-growing zoo of LLMs and benchmarks, the need to orchestrate multiple models for improved task performance has never been more pressing. While frameworks like Mixture-of-Agents (MoA) attempt to coordinate LLMs, they often fall short in terms of (1) selecting relevant agents, (2) facilitating effective intra-agent communication, and (3) integrating responses efficiently. In this work, we propose Graph-of-Agents (GoA), a new graph-based framework for modeling multi-agent LLM communication. Our approach begins with node sampling, selecting only the most relevant agents by leveraging model cards that summarize each model's domain, task specialization, and other characteristics. Next, we construct edges between the selected agents by evaluating their responses against one another to determine relevance ordering. Directed message passing is then performed from highly relevant agents to less relevant ones to enhance their responses, followed by reverse message passing to refine the original responses of the more relevant agents. Finally, the updated responses are aggregated via graph-based pooling (e.g., max or mean pooling) to produce a single, unified answer. We evaluate GoA on diverse multi-domain benchmarks (MMLU, MMLU-Pro, GPQA) and domain-specific benchmarks (MATH, HumanEval, MedMCQA), with an agent pool of 6 LLMs spanning multiple domains. Surprisingly, GoA achieves superior performance using only 3 selected agents, outperforming recent multi-agent LLM baselines that utilize all 6 agents simultaneously. By adopting a graph structure, GoA offers both scalability and effectiveness through structured message passing-positioning it as a strong candidate for navigating the challenges of the ever-growing LLM zoo. Code is available at: https://github.com/UNITES-Lab/GoA.
2604.17148.paper_summary_zh: 隨著 LLM 和基準測試的數量不斷增加，協調多個模型以改善任務表現的需求從未如此迫切。雖然像 Mixture-of-Agents (MoA) 這樣的框架試圖協調 LLM，但在 (1) 選擇相關代理、(2) 促進有效的代理內部通信，以及 (3) 高效整合回應方面，往往未能達到預期。在這項工作中，我們提出了 Graph-of-Agents (GoA)，這是一種基於圖的框架，用於建模多代理 LLM 通信。我們的方法從節點取樣開始，僅選擇最相關的代理，利用模型卡來總結每個模型的領域、任務專業化和其他特徵。接下來，我們通過評估所選代理之間的回應來構建邊，以確定相關性排序。然後，從高度相關的代理向較不相關的代理進行定向消息傳遞，以增強它們的回應，隨後進行反向消息傳遞以細化更相關代理的原始回應。最後，通過基於圖的池化（例如，最大或平均池化）來聚合更新的回應，以生成單一的統一答案。我們在多樣的多領域基準測試（MMLU、MMLU-Pro、GPQA）和特定領域基準測試（MATH、HumanEval、MedMCQA）上評估 GoA，代理池由 6 個涵蓋多個領域的 LLM 組成。令人驚訝的是，GoA 只使用 3 個選定的代理就達到了優越的性能，超越了最近利用所有 6 個代理的多代理 LLM 基準。通過採用圖結構，GoA 提供了可擴展性和有效性，通過結構化的消息傳遞使其成為應對不斷增長的 LLM 動物園挑戰的有力候選者。代碼可在以下網址獲取：https://github.com/UNITES-Lab/GoA。
2604.17148.author: Sukwon Yun et.al.
2604.17148.authors: Sukwon Yun, Jie Peng, Pingzhi Li, Wendong Fan, Jie Chen, James Zou, Guohao Li, Tianlong Chen
